us-politics.headline: Trump Warns Iran, Calls Ceasefire Offer 'Not Good Enough'
us-politics.body:
President Trump escalated pressure on Iran this week, warning the country could be 'taken out in one night' and threatening strikes on power plants and bridges. He acknowledged Iran submitted a 'significant' ceasefire proposal but rejected it as insufficient, while extending his own deadline - a move former National Security Advisor John Bolton says signals to Tehran that Trump is not serious.

A House Republican predicted troop deployment is likely, while a senior GOP defense voice argued the U.S. can reopen the Strait of Hormuz without a ground invasion. Senator Bernie Sanders dismissed Trump's threats as the 'ravings of a dangerous and mentally unbalanced individual.' One Hill opinion piece warned that Iran could effectively win any prolonged conflict simply by not losing - drawing parallels to Vietnam and Afghanistan.

sources.title: Live updates: Trump warns all of Iran ‘could be taken out in one night’ as he sticks to deadline
Bolton: Trump extending deadline shows Iran ‘he’s not really serious’
Sanders on Trump Iran f-bomb threat: ‘Ravings of a dangerous and mentally unbalanced individual’
Iran could defeat the US just by not losing
House Republican predicts Trump will deploy troops in Iran: ‘I just don’t see any other way’
Trump: Iran ceasefire proposal ‘significant,’ ‘not good enough’
Top GOP defense voice breaks with invasion fears, says US can reopen Hormuz without ground war
Trump reiterates threats to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges
Trump reveals Iran made 'significant proposal' after ultimatum, but 'not good enough'

us-politics.headline: U.S. Rescues Downed Airman From Iran in Covert Mission
us-politics.body:
American forces carried out a high-risk operation to recover a wounded airman shot down over Iran, with Trump publicly touting the mission's success. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth drew religious comparisons, calling the rescued pilot 'a pilot reborn' and linking the mission to the Easter story - framing that drew both praise from supporters and criticism from those who saw it as inappropriate.

Trump separately vowed to pursue whoever leaked details of the operation to the press, raising press freedom concerns. Former CIA Director David Petraeus, meanwhile, assessed that Russia no longer holds the upper hand in Ukraine - a separate but related signal about shifting U.S. military posture abroad.

sources.title: Hegseth ties Iran rescue to Easter story and Jesus Christ: 'A pilot reborn'
Former CIA director: ‘Russia no longer has the upper hand’ in Ukraine war
Trump says US will go after ‘leaker’ of info on downed pilot in Iran
Trump touts airman rescue mission, boasts Iran could be 'taken out in 1 night'
‘God is good’: Inside the high-risk US mission to save a wounded airman shot down in Iran

us-politics.headline: Supreme Court Backs Bannon; Flynn Settlement Faces Scrutiny
us-politics.body:
The Supreme Court sided with Steve Bannon in his bid to dismiss his January 6 contempt conviction, a significant legal win for one of Trump's closest allies. The ruling adds to a series of legal developments reshaping accountability cases tied to the Capitol riot.

Separately, Representative Jamie Raskin demanded the Justice Department hand over details of a settlement reached with Michael Flynn, raising questions about whether the DOJ is offering favorable treatment to Trump allies. Republican Representative Don Bacon also broke with the administration, calling Hegseth's firing of senior military officers 'not morally right nor wise' - a rare public rebuke from within the GOP.

sources.title: Supreme Court sides with Steve Bannon in bid to dismiss Jan. 6 conviction
Raskin demands DOJ turn over details on settlement with Michael Flynn
Don Bacon on Hegseth firing top military officers: ‘Not morally right nor wise’

us-politics.headline: AP Cuts Staff; CBS Replaces Colbert; OpenAI Proposes AI Rules
us-politics.body:
The Associated Press announced it is cutting dozens of staffers as part of a restructuring, continuing a broader wave of layoffs hitting legacy news organizations. CBS confirmed that Byron Allen's 'Comics Unleashed' will take over Stephen Colbert's Late Show time slot after Colbert's departure.

On the technology front, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman released a policy blueprint calling for new taxes and regulations on artificial intelligence - a notable move given the industry's historical resistance to oversight. Federal regulators also closed their investigation into Tesla's 'Actually Smart Summon' feature, clearing the company of safety concerns tied to the semi-autonomous parking tool.

sources.title: Associated Press cutting dozens of staffers
CBS announces what’s taking over Colbert’s ‘Late Show’ time slot
OpenAI’s Altman releases blueprint for taxing, regulating artificial intelligence
Regulator closes investigation into Tesla ‘actually smart summon’ feature

au-politics.headline: Cyclone Maila Bears Down on Far North Queensland
au-politics.body:
Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila is forecast to cross far north Queensland, threatening a region still recovering from Cyclone Narelle just three weeks ago. Emergency services are urging residents to prepare now as the system intensifies offshore.

The back-to-back cyclone threat puts pressure on local infrastructure and emergency response capacity already stretched by the earlier event. Authorities have not yet confirmed a precise landfall location, but the far north coast remains in the danger zone. Communities from Cairns northward are on alert.

sources.title: Tropical Cyclone Maila likely to cross Far North Queensland
Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila could hit far north Queensland three weeks after Narelle tore through

au-politics.headline: First Nations Voters Questioned on Identity at SA Polling Booths
au-politics.body:
Aboriginal voters in South Australia report being asked to verify their Indigenous identity at polling booths during elections for the state's Voice to Parliament, with some describing the experience as humiliating. Phrases like 'are you sure?' were reportedly used by electoral officials checking rolls.

Critics are calling for a formal review of the process, arguing it singles out First Nations people in a way that no other voter group faces. Supporters of the current system say roll integrity checks are standard practice. The reports reignite debate about how Indigenous-specific electoral processes are administered and whether existing safeguards create new forms of discrimination.

sources.title: 'Are you sure?': First Nations voters asked about identity at polling booths

au-politics.headline: Murdoch Son-in-Law Funds Independent; AACo Escapes Cattle Probe
au-politics.body:
Rupert Murdoch's pro-climate son-in-law Leopoldo Alejandro Betancourt Lopez is backing the independent candidate in the NSW seat of Farrer, a safe Nationals electorate. The funding adds an unusual dimension to the teal-style independent push in regional Australia, given its connection to the Murdoch family.

Separately, the Queensland government has ruled out investigating Australian Agricultural Company after the cattle giant provided assurances to regulators following the deaths of livestock from thirst. Critics say the decision lets a major agribusiness avoid scrutiny over animal welfare failures. The government argues the company's response was sufficient. Both stories raise questions about accountability - for political donors and for corporate actors - ahead of the federal election.

sources.title: Pro-climate son-in-law to Rupert Murdoch backs Farrer independent
AACo spared investigation into cattle thirst deaths after reassuring regulators

au-politics.headline: AI Reshapes Jobs and Environmental Rules as Risks Mount
au-politics.body:
Artificial intelligence is already cutting tech sector workforces, with companies reducing junior and mid-level roles as AI tools absorb tasks once done by humans. The shift is accelerating faster than many workers anticipated, and retraining pathways remain unclear.

Scientists are separately warning that using AI to prepare and assess environmental impact statements risks repeating the errors of the robodebt scandal - automated decisions made at scale with inadequate human oversight. The concern is that flawed AI outputs could approve harmful projects or reject valid ones before anyone notices the pattern. Both developments point to the same underlying tension: AI is moving faster than the regulatory and social frameworks designed to manage it.

sources.title: Tech starts to eat itself as AI reshapes workforce
Using AI to prepare and evaluate environmental assessments risks ‘robodebt-style’ failures, scientists say

world.headline: US-Israeli Strikes on Iran Intensify as Hormuz Deadline Looms
world.body:
US and Israeli forces have struck targets across Iran including homes, a major university, and infrastructure sites, with Defense Secretary Hegseth confirming strikes are increasing. Trump has called his deadline for Iran to halt its nuclear program final, while Iran has pushed a counter-proposal to end hostilities. The strikes have left rescue teams searching rubble for survivors in residential areas.

Iranians are bracing for further attacks on power plants and bridges as Trump threatens to destroy critical infrastructure. Democrats have condemned the campaign as potential war crimes; Republicans are broadly supportive. North Korea, meanwhile, is keeping distance from Tehran, according to South Korean intelligence. A US airman was rescued from Iranian territory during the chaos, an operation Trump publicly celebrated at a White House Easter event.

sources.title: Rescuers search for survivors after strikes hit homes in Iran
North Korea keeping Iran at arm’s length, reports Seoul
How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
Hegseth says strikes on Iran increasing as Hormuz deadline looms
Video: Iran’s top university hit in US-Israeli strikes
Democrats blast Trump for Iran ‘war crimes’ threat; Republicans supportive
Trump warns deadline ‘final’ as Iran pushes proposal to end war
Trump praised airman’s rescue in Iran during White House Easter event
'We're sinking deeper': Iranians brace for infrastructure strikes as Trump deadline nears
Where are Iran’s power plants that Trump has threatened to destroy?
In maps and photos: Five Iranian civilian bridges at risk of US strikes

world.headline: Gaza and Lebanon Bleed as Israeli Offensive Widens
world.body:
Israeli strikes killed at least ten people in Gaza in the past 24 hours, with fighting also erupting between Hamas and a rival militia. An Israeli army vehicle fired on a WHO convoy in southern Gaza, killing one aid worker. A grandmother described losing her entire family to the bombardment. Children in displacement camps are receiving psychological support as the humanitarian situation deteriorates.

In Lebanon, renewed Israeli strikes are accompanying a deepening ground offensive. The dual-front campaign is stretching Israeli military operations across two borders simultaneously, with civilian casualties mounting in both territories.

sources.title: Renewed Israeli strikes pound Lebanon as ground offensive deepens
Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say
Gaza camp offers children a sense of safety
Israeli army fire on WHO vehicle in southern Gaza kills one, medics report
Gaza grandmother loses her family due to Israeli attacks

world.headline: Global Energy Crisis Deepens as Conflicts Choke Supply Routes
world.body:
The war in Iran and the standoff over the Strait of Hormuz are sending shockwaves through global energy markets, with Asia bearing the sharpest impact as oil supply routes face disruption. Libya's internal oil disputes are compounding the crisis, stoking energy security fears across Europe. Cairo has begun dimming city lights as electricity costs soar.

Ukraine struck the Russian Black Sea energy hub at Novorossiysk, adding further pressure to an already strained global supply chain. The convergence of conflicts across the Middle East and Eastern Europe is pushing energy prices to levels that are reshaping daily life from Egypt to East Asia.

sources.title: How Asia became ground zero for the oil crisis
Cairo faces darker nights as global energy prices continue to soar
Libya’s oil disputes mirror Hormuz crisis, fuel European energy fears
Ukraine strikes Russian Black Sea energy hub Novorossiysk

world.headline: Russia Escalates Ukraine Strikes; Artemis II Sets Distance Record
world.body:
Russia has ramped up aerial strikes on Ukraine, with rescue teams pulling a child from rubble following the latest wave of attacks. The intensification comes as Ukraine conducts its own long-range strike on Novorossiysk, targeting Russian energy infrastructure on the Black Sea.

On a brighter note, NASA's Artemis II crew has broken the Apollo 13 record for the farthest distance humans have traveled from Earth, marking a milestone in the return to deep space exploration. Elsewhere, Myanmar's junta chief has been formally elected president, raising questions about the country's political trajectory, and South Korea reports that Kim Jong Un's daughter is now considered the most likely successor to lead North Korea.

sources.title: Child rescued from rubble after Russia ramps up strikes on Ukraine
Artemis II breaks Apollo 13 record for farthest human travel from Earth
North Korea: Kim's daughter now seen as likely heir — South
Myanmar junta chief elected president — what comes next?

ai.headline: Iran Threatens Stargate AI Data Centers in Abu Dhabi
ai.body:
Iran has issued threats against OpenAI's Stargate data centers in the UAE, raising security concerns for one of the most ambitious AI infrastructure projects underway. The threats come as Stargate - the joint venture backed by OpenAI, SoftBank, and Oracle - pushes to build out massive compute capacity across the Middle East and beyond.

The timing is awkward for OpenAI, which this week also published a policy vision calling for public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day work week to manage AI's economic disruption. The company is simultaneously pitching a utopian AI future while its physical infrastructure faces real-world geopolitical risk.

sources.title: Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers
Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi
OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day work week

ai.headline: Offline AI Speech Tools Arrive From Google and Indie Developers
ai.body:
Google quietly launched an offline-first AI dictation app for iOS, bringing on-device speech-to-text to users without requiring a cloud connection. The move signals growing demand for private, local AI processing - and Google is not alone. An independent developer separately released Ghost Pepper, a hold-to-talk speech-to-text tool for macOS that runs entirely on-device.

Both tools reflect a clear shift in user expectations. People increasingly want AI capabilities that do not send audio to remote servers. Local inference is fast enough now that the cloud is no longer a prerequisite for quality transcription.

sources.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
Show HN: Ghost Pepper – 100% local hold-to-talk speech-to-text for macOS

ai.headline: Vibe Coding Backlash Grows as Claude Code Draws Complaints
ai.body:
A sharp essay calling the vibe coding movement "insane" gained traction this week, arguing that AI-assisted coding is producing a generation of developers who ship without understanding what they built. The critique lands as Anthropic faces a separate, concrete problem: users are reporting that Claude Code became significantly less capable for complex engineering tasks after a February update.

The two stories together paint a messy picture for AI coding tools. Enthusiasm is high, but reliability is uneven - and the gap between demo and production use remains wide. Freestyle, a new startup offering sandboxed environments for AI coding agents, launched this week betting that infrastructure, not the models themselves, is the missing piece.

sources.title: The cult of vibe coding is insane
Claude Code is unusable for complex engineering tasks with the Feb updates
Launch HN: Freestyle: Sandboxes for AI Coding Agents

ai.headline: Adobe's Hosts File Manipulation Fuels User Privacy Anger
ai.body:
Adobe has been caught silently modifying the system hosts file on user machines to detect whether Creative Cloud is installed - a behavior users and security researchers are calling deceptive and overreaching. The hosts file is a sensitive system resource, and altering it without clear disclosure crosses a line many consider fundamental to software trust.

The backlash is fierce. For a company already under scrutiny over its subscription practices and data-use policies, this is another self-inflicted wound. It reinforces a broader pattern of enterprise software vendors treating user machines as extensions of their own infrastructure.

sources.title: Adobe modifies hosts file to detect whether Creative Cloud is installed

btc.headline: Bitcoin Hovers Near $70K as Geopolitics Drive Swings
btc.body:
Bitcoin climbed above $70,000 Monday before profit-taking pulled it back, with Trump's ultimatum to Iran driving much of the volatility. Reports of ceasefire talks sparked a brief rally, while prediction markets emerged as real-time gauges of war risk - with traders watching Iran bets as closely as any macro indicator.

The options market tells a more cautious story. Traders are quietly pricing significant downside protection, even as contrarian signals suggest a potential bottom is forming. Analysts see room to rally but flag profit-taking pressure and macro uncertainty as the key obstacles.

Strategy added another 4,871 BTC for $330 million, pushing holdings near 767,000 BTC despite paper losses topping $14.5 billion in Q1. The buy signals institutional conviction remains intact, whatever the short-term noise.

sources.title: Bitcoin options market is quietly pricing a major downside move
Can Trump’s ultimatum to Iran push Bitcoin price back up to $75K?
Bitcoin climbs above $70,000 as more contrarian bottoming signs emerge
Bitcoin profit taking keeps BTC below $70K as Trump doubles down on Iran
Strategy adds $330M BTC as paper losses top $14.5B in Q1
Strategy added another 4,871 bitcoin for $330 million, with holdings nearing 767,000 BTC
Bitcoin has room to rally, but there's a catch
Bitcoin rallies on report of Iran ceasefire talks, Algorand extends gains
First real bull signal since 2025? Five things to know in Bitcoin this week
Iran war bets turn prediction markets into real-time macro radar: Sygnum

btc.headline: Dimon Warns JPMorgan Must Move Faster on Tokenization
btc.body:
Jamie Dimon used JPMorgan's annual letter to flag blockchain and stablecoins as genuine competitive threats, pointing to the bank's own Kinexys platform as evidence the firm is moving - but warning it needs to move faster. The IMF echoed the urgency from a different angle, cautioning that tokenization could drag crypto-market risks into the heart of global finance.

Bernstein analysts argued tokenized credit could double Figure Technology's stock price, while China's tax authority pushed banks to adopt blockchain for lending. The institutional embrace of tokenization is accelerating on multiple fronts simultaneously.

The tension is clear: legacy finance sees tokenization as both opportunity and existential threat, while regulators are still working out whether the plumbing is safe enough to carry systemic weight.

sources.title: JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon sees ‘new competitors’ from blockchain,  stablecoins
Figure Technology's tokenized credit platform could help double stock price: Bernstein
Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan must move faster as tokenization reshapes finance
China's tax authority urges banks to implement blockchain for lending services
IMF warns tokenization could bring crypto risks into global financial markets

btc.headline: US Crypto Legislation Gets April Timeline, Aave Loses Risk Manager
btc.body:
A US Senate Banking Committee member confirmed an April target for crypto market structure legislation, giving the industry its clearest regulatory timeline yet. The news arrives as the sector pushes hard for rules that would clarify which assets are securities and which are commodities.

Inside DeFi, Aave is dealing with a messier situation. Chaos Labs, one of its key risk managers, has departed amid a broader contributor exodus and unresolved disputes - raising questions about governance stability at one of the protocol's most critical junctures.

Meanwhile, Polymarket launched a full exchange upgrade to take direct control of its trading infrastructure and data feeds, and an appeals court blocked New Jersey from shutting down Kalshi's sports prediction markets. Prediction platforms are quietly accumulating both technical and legal wins.

sources.title: Aave loses key risk manager, Chaos Labs, amid contributor exodus and disputes
US Senate Banking panel member confirms April timeline for crypto market structure
Polymarket reveals a 'full exchange upgrade' to take control of its own trading and truth
Appeals court blocks New Jersey from shutting down Kalshi's sports markets

btc.headline: AI Deepfakes Target Crypto KYC as Quantum Risk Debate Heats Up
btc.body:
A new AI-powered cybercrime tool is exploiting deepfakes to defeat KYC systems at crypto exchanges and banks, adding a sharp edge to concerns about identity verification in an era of synthetic media. The threat is practical and immediate, not theoretical.

On a longer time horizon, Samson Mow warned that rushing a quantum-computing fix into Bitcoin could backfire badly, arguing that a poorly implemented patch poses more danger than the quantum threat itself. The debate reflects a broader tension between acting early and acting carefully on low-probability, high-impact risks.

Elsewhere, a solo Bitcoin miner beat 1-in-28,000 odds to claim a $210,000 block reward - a reminder that the network's fundamentals keep ticking regardless of the noise above.

sources.title: Solo bitcoin miner overcomes 1-in-28,000 odds to secure $210,000 block reward
New AI cybercrime tool targets crypto, bank KYC systems via deepfakes
Rushed quantum fix may backfire for Bitcoin, Samson Mow warns

food.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod
food.description: Silky, caramelised fish fillets marinated in white miso, mirin, and sake then broiled until lacquered and golden
food.tip: Marinate for at least 24 hours — the longer the miso sits on the fish, the deeper the umami crust when it hits the heat

exercise.name: The Viking Row
exercise.description: Six hard 3-minute rowing intervals at near-maximum effort, each separated by 90 seconds of slow recovery rowing, bookended by a 2-minute warm-up and cool-down — brutal on the lungs, kind to the joints

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same object — philosophers have extended this to human cells, memories, and consciousness itself
learn.why: On a Monday in early April it is oddly grounding to sit with the question of whether the you who woke up this morning is the same person who went to sleep last night — Derek Parfit's modern take in 'Reasons and Persons' will genuinely unsettle your assumptions about continuity and what you owe your future self
